children.description: Delete the operational IAM user. Default mode opens with a Pulumi-residue check and refuses if any AWS substrate stack (`aws-eks`, `aws-eks-subzone`, `aws-test`, `aws-ses`) still owns live resources. `--destroy-pulumi-residue` orchestrates `prodbox aws stack <stack> destroy --yes` in canonical order before deleting the operational IAM user; destroying the long-lived `aws-ses` stack triggers a 5-30 min SES re-verification and ~24h S3 bucket-name cooldown. `--allow-pulumi-residue` is the operator-acknowledged recovery escape hatch that bypasses the residue check entirely (stacks become orphaned). `--destroy-pulumi-residue` and `--allow-pulumi-residue` are mutually exclusive at parse time.

examples.exampleDescription: Delete the operational IAM user (refuses if any AWS substrate stack is live).
Destroy live AWS substrate stacks in canonical order, then delete the operational IAM user.

children.name: teardown

options.description: Render the IAM teardown plan without mutating state
Write the rendered plan to a file
Run `prodbox aws stack <stack> destroy --yes` for each live Pulumi-managed AWS stack in canonical order before deleting the operational IAM user (mutually exclusive with --allow-pulumi-residue)

Bypass the Pulumi-residue refuse-path that prevents deleting the operational IAM user while AWS substrate stacks still have live resources (operator-acknowledged recovery only; mutually exclusive with --destroy-pulumi-residue)

children.description: Reconcile the supported local cluster state. Local-only by default (no AWS); pass --with-edge to also reconcile the public edge (or run `prodbox edge reconcile` separately).

examples.exampleDescription: Reconcile the supported local cluster (no AWS needed).
Reconcile the cluster and attach the public edge.

children.name: reconcile

options.description: Render the lifecycle plan without mutating state
Write the rendered plan to a file
Also reconcile the AWS-gated public edge (Route 53 DNS + ZeroSSL TLS); requires operational aws.*

children.description: Delete the local cluster. Default mode opens with a per-run-residue check and refuses if `aws-eks`, `aws-eks-subzone`, or `aws-test` still have live resources. `--cascade` orchestrates the full teardown (K8s drain + per-run Pulumi destroys + cluster uninstall + postflight tag sweep) as one atomic operator action; the K8s drain phase skips gracefully when no cluster is reachable. `--allow-pulumi-residue` bypasses the residue check for operator-acknowledged recovery scenarios. `--cascade` and `--allow-pulumi-residue` are mutually exclusive at parse time.

examples.exampleDescription: Delete the local cluster (refuses if per-run Pulumi stacks are live).
Orchestrate the full teardown as one atomic operator action.

children.name: delete

options.description: Confirm full cluster deletion
Orchestrate the full teardown (K8s drain + per-run Pulumi destroys + cluster uninstall + postflight tag sweep) as one atomic operator action
Bypass the per-run Pulumi residue refuse-path (recovery only)
Render the delete plan without mutating state
Write the rendered plan to a file
